StreamLineProgrammable Micropayments on Sui

$0.00transfer fee on every drip
≤ 60ssettlement frequency
5on-chain state machine
1 bpskeeper tip per drip
USDC◆USDsui◆SuiUSDe◆USDY◆FDUSD◆AUSD◆USDB◆Address Balances◆Programmable Transaction Blocks◆Move Object Model◆zkLogin◆Scallop◆NAVI◆USDC◆USDsui◆SuiUSDe◆USDY◆FDUSD◆AUSD◆USDB◆Address Balances◆Programmable Transaction Blocks◆Move Object Model◆zkLogin◆Scallop◆NAVI◆

05 · How it works

A state machine enforced by Move — not a bypassable require().

LOCKEDwaiting
→
PENDINGraised
→
DRIPPINGapproved
→
PAUSEDdispute
→
DONEexhausted
PhaseActorOn-chain actionState after
SetupClientcreate_stream() — locks full amountLOCKED
Signal doneFreelancerraise_completion() — sets review deadlinePENDING_REVIEW
ReviewClientapprove_milestone() via StreamCapDRIPPING
Auto-approveKeeperapprove_milestone() after 48h deadlineDRIPPING
SettlementKeeperdrip() — send_funds() gasless per splitDRIPPING
DisputeClientraise_dispute() — stream pausesPAUSED
CompletionProtocolall milestones exhaustedDONE

The auto-approve timer protects the freelancer: if a client goes silent after a milestone is raised, the keeper calls approve_milestone() after the deadline (default 48h). Silence equals approval.

14 · Why this is Sui-native

Why does this exist on Sui and not Ethereum or Solana?

01

Address Balances

Zero-fee transfers at protocol level via 0x2::balance::send_funds — not a subsidy or relayer. Enables true per-interval streaming at any payment size. On Ethereum every drip costs gas.

02

PTB atomicity

The split (pay + save + invest) executes as one atomic transaction. If the yield-routing leg fails, the whole drip reverts — protecting split integrity.

03

Move object ownership

The locked Balance lives inside a shared object, reachable only through entry functions. A structural property of Move's type system — no reentrancy, no approval manipulation.

04

zkLogin

Google OAuth produces a Sui address directly. No seed phrase, no wallet install. A freelancer signs up with Gmail and immediately receives streams.

15 · Competitive landscape

The first streaming protocol where the money genuinely arrives.

ProtocolChainGaslessMilestonesSplit autoCollateral
SablierEthereumNoNoNoNo
SuperfluidEthereum/L2NoNoNoNo
Drips (Radicle)EthereumNoNoNoNo
LlamaPayMulti-EVMNoNoNoNo
StreamLineSuiYesYesYesYes

Every EVM streaming protocol requires recipients to periodically claim accumulated balance — a gas-paying action. On StreamLine the money lands continuously, with no action required and no fee for either party.

StreamLine

Zero fees. No wallet setup. Pays as you work. This is what programmable money looks like on Sui.

launch app →
